commit.message:
Add IPFS snapshot import & merge by CID (#20)

* Initial plan

* feat: add IPFS snapshot import/merge feature by CID

- Add mergeSnapshotData() to fitnessData.js: merges two data sets, deduplicating by timestamp, sorting chronologically, handling both modern exercises {types,entries} and legacy array formats
- Add importAndMergeFromCID() to fitnessData.js: fetches snapshot from IPFS gateway by CID, validates structure, merges into local data, tracks imported CIDs in localStorage
- Import importAndMergeFromCID in app.js
- Add '📥 Import' button to IPFS hover popup alongside existing 'Show All'
- Add showImportSnapshotModal() with CID input, feedback, previously-imported history
- Show imported snapshot CIDs section in showAllSnapshotsModal()

commit.message:
Fix stack-buffer-overflow: guard multiplier[] access for free variables in dual ratio test

When statusArray[seq] & 3 == 0 (free or superbasic variable), the expression
(statusArray[seq] & 3) - 1 evaluates to -1.  The guard 'if (iStatus)' passes
for -1 (non-zero), causing 'multiplier[iStatus - 1]' = 'multiplier[-2]' — an
out-of-bounds stack read.

ASan reported this as a stack-buffer-overflow in
ClpPackedMatrix::transposeTimesByColumn at ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:1043,
triggered by instance neos-911970 in a parallel hwpar build.

Fix all affected sites that use the 2-element multiplier[] = {-1.0, 1.0}
pattern with [iStatus/wanted - 1] indexing:

- ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:1042  (confirmed ASan hit): if (iStatus) -> if (iStatus > 0)
- ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:8356  (dualColumn0, slack loop): same fix
- ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:8421  (dualColumn0, column loop): same fix
- ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:10026 (#ifndef COIN_AVX2 path): same fix
- ClpPackedMatrix.cpp:1771,1966 (gutsOfTransposeTimesUnscaled): keep output
  vector population for free columns but guard ratio test with if (wanted > 0)
- ClpSimplexDual.cpp:2395   (updateDualBit, #if ABOCA_LITE): keep reducedCost
  update for free vars, guard multiplier access with if (iStatus > 0)
- ClpSimplexDual.cpp:3535   (dualColumn0): if (wanted) -> if (wanted > 0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix publish to GitHub: use repo full name instead of numeric ID (#111)

* Fix publish to GitHub: use repo full name instead of numeric ID

PublishSecretsAsync and GitHubActionsPublisher.ParseRepositoryId expect
'owner/repo' format, but RepositoryId was being set from repo.Id which
is the numeric GitHub repository ID (e.g., 463927594). Fixed all places
that set RepositoryId to use repo.FullName instead, and changed the
publish call to pass RepositoryFullName. Also fixed dedup checks to
compare on FullName for consistency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix NullReferenceException after publishing secrets

The wizard closed immediately after publish completed (before user
clicked Done), returning null. SecretsPublish.razor then did
'if (result)' which unboxed null to bool causing NullReferenceException.

Two fixes:
- Set PreventSubmitClose=true during publish so the modal stays open
  on the results step until user clicks Done
- Change 'if (result)' to 'if (result == true)' for null safety

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix publish not respecting key mappings

The ResolveSecretsAsync method was using SanitizeLabel(Label) to generate
default key names, but the profile editor's GetDefaultKeys() uses the
Platform and DistributionType enums. This caused a mismatch — e.g. label
'iOS App Store' produced 'IOS_APP_STORE' via SanitizeLabel but 'IOS_APPSTORE'
from the enum. The mapping lookup then failed and the unmapped default key
was used instead.

Also fixed installer cert keys (_INSTALLER_CERTIFICATE_P12 vs _INSTALLER_P12)
and provisioning profile keys (_PROVISIONING_PROFILE vs _PROFILE) to match
the keys that GetDefaultKeys() generates.

Fixed the resolve-warning detection in PublishReviewModal to use the same
enum-based prefix matching instead of label-based substring matching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Docs: mark Stage 7f complete, update status to 7g next

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Stage 7f: cook pass (nomm_t → nomm1_t) + run_nomm1 interpreter

- Export hot_lookup() and sock_match() from nock.h/nock.c for use by ska.c
- Add nomm1_t.n2.ax field for nock_op9_continue arm dispatch
- Implement cook_nomm(): structural conversion nomm_t → nomm1_t
  - NOMM_DS2 → NOMM_2 {has_bell, bell, jet=cook_find_jet()}
  - NOMM_I2  → NOMM_2 {q=formula_formula, has_bell=false}
  - NOMM_9   → NOMM_2 {has_bell=false} (conservative fallback)
  - NOMM_DUS2 → NOMM_2 {q, has_bell}
  - All linear opcodes: structural recursion
- Implement run_nomm1(): NOMM_2 dispatch priority:
  1. Static jet (pre-wired O(1) via cook_find_jet + sock_match at cook time)
  2. Indirect (q != NULL): runtime formula eval via nock_ex
  3. Direct (DS2/NOMM_9): nock_op9_continue for dynamic jet dispatch
- Implement ska_analyze(): scan + frond validation + cook → boil_t*
- Update ska_nock() to use cook + run_nomm1 (eval_nomm fallback on alloc failure)
- Update ROADMAP.md: 7a–7e ✅ DONE, 7f 🔄 in progress → mark 7f done
- Update AGENTS.md: Phase 7 stage table + phase plan summary

177/177 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Three install methods each: marketplace, direct, and manual project-level

commit.message:
Fix exercise log displaying "0 reps x [object Object] sets" on first submission (#18)

* Initial plan

* Fix exercise logger showing 0 reps and [object Object] sets on first log

The exercise form stores entries with `sets` as an array of per-set
{reps, weight} objects, but displayRecentExercises() assumed `reps` and
`sets` were plain numbers. This caused first-time form-submitted exercises
to display "0 reps x [object Object] sets".

- Add getEntryTotalReps() helper at the top of displayRecentExercises()
  to handle both storage formats
- Use helper in the log entry list display (fixing the [object Object] bug)
- Use helper in pullup/pushup/situp tally computations so form-submitted
  entries are counted correctly too

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: DA1/DA2 クエリの送信を根本的に抑止する (#103)

* fix: DA1/DA2 クエリの送信を根本的に抑止する

【新たに判明した問題】
「61;4;6;7;14;21;22;23;24;28;32;42;52c」(DA1 応答)・
「0;10;1c」(DA2 応答) が stdin に混入していた。
これは vim が DA2 クエリ (\e[>c) を送信していたことが原因。

【変更内容】
- dot_vimrc: t_RV・t_RB・t_RF を空に設定し vim のクエリを全て無効化
  - t_RV=: DA2（バージョン問い合わせ）クエリを送信させない
  - t_RB=: OSC 11（背景色）クエリを送信させない（set background=dark と二重対策）
  - t_RF=: OSC 10（前景色）クエリを送信させない
- dot_tmux.conf: default-terminal を tmux-256color に切り替え（条件付き）
  - tmux-256color は terminfo が完全で、プログラムが DA クエリで機能補完する動作を抑制
  - infocmp で存在確認後に切り替え、利用不可の場合は screen-256color にフォールバック
- dot_zshrc / dot_bashrc: ドレイン関数を2フェーズに改善
  - フェーズ1: 非ブロッキック即時チェック（データなし時は遅延ゼロ）
  - フェーズ2: データがあった場合のみ20ms待機ループで残りも全て捕捉
  - 起動時ドレインを末尾に追加（全ツールロード後に100ms待機して応答を破棄）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: terminal-overrides Tc を terminal-features RGB に置き換える

【変更理由】
terminal-overrides の Tc 設定は tmux がアタッチ時に外部ターミナルへ
OSC 10/11 クエリを送信する原因となっていた。
応答 (`\e]11;rgb:xxxx/xxxx/xxxx\a`) がタイミングによってシェルの
stdin に漏れ込み `^[]11;rgb:0c0c/0c0c/0c0c^[` として表示されていた。

terminal-features の RGB は宣言型（クエリなし）でサポートを有効化するため
アタッチ時の OSC 10/11 クエリを回避できる（tmux 3.2+ 対応）。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: 起動時ドレインを opt-in 化しユーザー先行入力の破棄を防止する

【変更内容】
- dot_bashrc / dot_zshrc の起動時ドレインをデフォルト無効に変更
  - bash: BASH_DRAIN_ESC_ON_STARTUP=1 を設定した場合のみ実行
  - zsh: ZSH_STARTUP_ESC_DRAIN を設定した場合のみ実行
- 起動時ドレインを -t 0（非ブロッキング）に変更
  - 以前の -t 0.1（100ms 待機）はユーザーの先行入力を破棄する恐れがあった
- コメントを「二層防衛」の役割分担として更新
  - PROMPT_COMMAND / precmd ドレインが主要手段
  - 起動時ドレインは opt-in の安全網

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: akubiusa <222856114+akubiusa@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ska): Stage 7d/7e — loop detection and memo cache

Stage 7e: loop detection (++close heuristic)
- Global scan state: fols_stack (analysis call chain), site_gen (monotone
  evalsite counter), g_block (blocked par/kid pairs), g_fronds (loop
  assumptions for validation)
- ska_pass_reset(): resets per-pass state; g_block persists across retries
- Case 9 of scan_cell: when arm formula is statically known (sock_pull gives
  KNOWN cape), searches fols_stack for same formula with sock_huge subsumption
  check; if loop detected emits NOMM_DS2 backedge (body=NULL); otherwise
  pushes frame, scans body, pops frame, emits NOMM_DS2 with body
- eval_nomm NOMM_DS2: body!=NULL → eval body with core as subject;
  body==NULL (backedge) → nock_op9_continue for jet dispatch/fallback
- ska_nock: redo-loop with frond validation; invalid fronds add to g_block
  and retry up to SKA_MAX_RETRIES times; exhausted → plain nock fallback
- New test: 'ska loop: recursive battery' — [9 2 [0 1]] battery exercises
  the backedge path; dec jet fires at nock_op9_continue

Stage 7d: memo cache
- g_memo[]: (formula, sub-sock, body, prod) entries, reset per pass
- In case 9: before scanning, check if same formula was analyzed for a
  more-general subject (sock_huge(memo.sub, core_sock)); if hit, reuse body
- After scan: store result in g_memo for future callers
- Two new tests verifying repeated dec(5) calls return 4

nock.h/nock.c: nock_op9_continue() added (used by NOMM_DS2 backedges and
the existing NOMM_9 path).

All 177 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update .github/instructions/*.instructions.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Ricardo M Martínez Rodriguez <rmartinez@applaudostudios.com>

commit.message:
Add observability: structured logging, OpenTelemetry, swarm dashboard

Structured logging:
- JSONFormatter for machine-parseable logs (SWARM_LOG_FORMAT=json)
- EventTracker records lifecycle events with timing and metadata
- configure_logging() replaces manual basicConfig

OpenTelemetry tracing:
- Optional OTLP span export for each lifecycle stage
- Enabled via OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var
- Install extras: pip install swarm-agent[otel]
- Spans include swarm.persona, swarm.repo, swarm.stage attributes

Swarm Dashboard CLI:
- python -m swarm_agent.dashboard owner/repo
- Queries GitHub labels to show active/completed agent status
- Watch mode (--watch) with configurable refresh interval
- JSON output (--format json) for scripting
- swarm-dashboard console_script entry point

Lifecycle metrics:
- Agent posts timing breakdown comment on issue/PR at completion
- Collapsible <details> block with per-stage durations
- Tracks errors encountered during run

66 tests passing, lint clean.

livekit: switch to UDP mux for Docker best practice

Replace the large 20000-21000 UDP port range with UDP mux (7882-7913,
32 ports matching 32 CPU threads). This avoids massive iptables overhead
from Docker bridge port publishing. The LiveKit config reference
recommends a port count >= the number of CPU threads for best
performance.

Updated MATRIX-RTC.md documentation and compose.matrix.yml ports.

commit.message:
feat(ska): Stage 7a/7b — types and cape/sock operations

src/ska.h: full SKA type definitions
  - cape_t: boolean subject-knowledge tree (CAPE_KNOWN/CAPE_WILD/cell)
  - sock_t: reuses nock.h definition; SKA uses same [cape data] struct
  - nomm_t: annotated Nock AST with %i2/%ds2/%dus2 Nock-2 variants
  - nomm1_t: cooked AST; all %2 sites carry bell_t + optional jet_fn_t
  - bell_t: call site identity [bus=sock fol=*]
  - site_t, glob_t: evalsite IDs and cross-arm labels
  - short_t: per-arm scan state (site counter, cycle stack, want map)
  - long_t: global analysis state (arm counter, memo table)
  - cycle_t: SCC tracking with frond list and melo cache
  - boil_t: cook pass output (entry nomm1 + site table)
  - ska_analyze() / run_nomm1() public API stubs declared

src/ska.c: Stage 7b cape/sock operations
  - cape_and, cape_or: intersection/union of knowledge trees
  - cape_pull: extract sub-cape at Nock axis (MSB-to-LSB traversal)
  - cape_head, cape_tail, cape_cons: tree navigation helpers
  - sock_pull: extract sub-sock at Nock axis (mirrors ++pull:so)
  - sock_huge: subsumption check a ⊇ b (mirrors ++huge:so)
  - sock_knit: autocons of two socks (mirrors ++knit:so)
  - sock_purr: intersection for %6 branches (mirrors ++purr:so)
  - sock_darn: tree edit at axis (mirrors ++darn:so / hax())
  - sock_dunno, sock_known: constructors
  - ska_arena: 256KB bump allocator for nomm_t/nomm1_t/boil_t

Makefile: add ska.o to OBJS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 12 mission-planning ES modules

New modules for launch planning, transfer design, phasing/RPO,
delta-V budgeting, mission sequencing, lunar transfer, and stubs
for Lambert solver, porkchop plots, and interplanetary transfers.

- js/launch-sites.js: Launch site data, azimuth, rotation benefit
- js/launch-vehicles.js: Vehicle classes, suitability, insertion dV
- js/launch-planner.js: Launch-to-orbit feasibility calculator
- js/window-search.js: Generic window search engine
- js/transfer-planner.js: Hohmann, bi-elliptic, combined transfers
- js/phasing.js: Phasing orbits and simplified RPO planning
- js/delta-v-budget.js: Mission delta-V budget builder
- js/mission-sequencer.js: Mission leg sequencing and timeline
- js/lunar-transfer.js: Earth-to-Moon transfer planning
- js/lambert.js: Lambert solver stub
- js/porkchop.js: Porkchop plot stub
- js/interplanetary.js: Interplanetary transfer stub

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
